checklist: Understand linear bounds bypassing comparison sorting limits
Implement Bucket and Radix Sort algorithms
Evaluate constraints on linear sorting ranges

states.question: What is the theoretical lower limit of comparison-based sorting runtimes?
states.explanation: A decision tree model for sorting N elements has N! leaves. The height of the tree is log(N!) which is Omega(N log N) minimum comparison steps.

states.analogy_description: If you sort letters by zip codes, you don't compare them. You look at the zip code and drop it directly into its assigned slot. This bypasses comparisons.

whiteboard_data.title: Counting Tally Array
whiteboard_data.description: Counting element occurrences to build sorted arrays.

states.exercise_description: Write a simple Counting Sort function `counting_sort_simple(lst, max_val)` that sorts a list of non-negative integers up to `max_val`.
Write a simple bucket sort `bucket_sort_simple(lst)` that sorts positive float numbers between 0 and 1 using 5 buckets.

states.prompt: Explain Linear Sorting in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.front: What does Radix Sort rely on?
flashcards.back: Stable sorting digit-by-digit from least significant to most significant.

flashcards.front: Can Counting Sort handle float numbers?
flashcards.back: No, it requires distinct integer indices to tally frequencies.
